data.description:
# Flaky: test_import_batch_resume._commit_count crashes on empty `git rev-list` output

## Context (discovered during CI for bug 7c91-0488)
On gerrit-verify run 29620766631 (ubuntu py3.12 only; py3.11/py3.13/macos all passed), the
integration tier failed:

    FAILED tests/interfaces/io/test_import_batch_resume.py::test_benchmark_commit_count_is_ceil_per_pass
    E   ValueError: invalid literal for int() with base 10: ''   (test_import_batch_resume.py:51)

## Root cause (suspected — not yet proven)
`_commit_count` (tests/interfaces/io/test_import_batch_resume.py:45-51) runs
`git rev-list --count HEAD` via subprocess.run WITHOUT check=True and WITHOUT guarding empty
stdout, then `int(r.stdout.strip())`. In the concurrency/integration tier a timing race (tracker
HEAD not yet present / transient git error) yields empty stdout -> int('') -> ValueError. Single-
cell matrix failure = flake signature.

## Suggested fix
Assert the subprocess return code and non-empty stdout (or retry) before int(); fail with the
git stderr rather than an opaque int('') ValueError. Out of scope for the terraform-drift fix.

## Acceptance criteria
- _commit_count no longer raises int('') on empty/failed `git rev-list`; it either retries to a
  ready HEAD or fails with a clear diagnostic including git's stderr.
- The test passes deterministically across the py3.11/3.12/3.13 matrix.
data.title: Flaky: test_import_batch_resume._commit_count int('') on empty git rev-list output
